nodes.label: Analytic & covariance readouts — the 2nd-pass audit
nodes.rationale: The rough Phase-7 plan fixed the bake-off on {linear-softmax, cosine, NCM, RanPAC} — spine-correct but incomplete; this audit widens it with the Analytic-CL/RLS family, FeCAM, and the RanDumb skeptic control.

nodes.label: Analytic Continual Learning / RLS family (headed by F-OAL)
nodes.rationale: Recasts CIL classifier training as recursive least squares: no exemplars, no gradient, no forgetting by construction; F-OAL is forward-only AND online AND analytic — our exact constraint, if any single published method is 'the namer we were about to reinvent' it's this one. Turns 'no-gradient streaming namer' from a hopeful bet into adopting a published paradigm.

nodes.label: FeCAM — per-class covariance Mahalanobis
nodes.rationale: Closed-form multimodality escape (per-class covariance instead of a non-convex mixture), SOTA exemplar-free CIL (CIFAR-100/5t 70.9% vs NCM 64.8%) — but it is the maximal-magnitude readout on the table (whitened Mahalanobis distance), the sharpest anti-spine pole, sharpening the spine-vs-accuracy tension for the bake-off.

nodes.label: RanDumb — the skeptic's control (does the trained bulk earn its keep)
nodes.rationale: A fixed random projection + simple classifier outperforms online-CL-learned representations on standard benchmarks; operationalizes the reservoir hedge as an experiment — run random-from-taps AND random-from-pixels controls; if the trained bulk doesn't beat these, the 80% SCFF isn't pulling its weight at the naming stage.

nodes.label: The widened bake-off taxonomy (gradient/direction-pure/magnitude/analytic/controls)
nodes.rationale: The headline Phase-7 question restated with the wider field: the strongest no-gradient namers are magnitude-based (FeCAM, RanPAC/RLS ridge); does the direction-pure cosine head match their accuracy x forgetting, and by how much do we pay to stay spine-clean, plus does anything beat RanDumb at all.
